titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O'Farrell to Patrick O'Donoghue, 2 June 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Edward O'Farrell (1857-1926), the assistant under-secretary for Ireland, to Patrick O'Donoghue (b. 1888). The letter refers to a pervious letter sent by O'Donoghue regarding the whereabouts of town councillor Patrick Joseph Ryan (b. 1881) following his arrest in relation to the 1916 Easter Rising. O'Farrell's letter states that O'Donoghue should make the enquiry at Richmond Barracks. Edward O'Farrell served as the assistant undersecretary of Ireland after the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5612/9461/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
9461. 5th June, 1916. Sir, I am directed by the Lords Justices to
acknowledge the receipt of your letter, and to
state that you should address the Provost
Marshal, Richmond Barracks, Dublin, respecting
the matter referred to therein. I am,
Sir,
Your Obedient Servant, E O'Farrell Mr. P.O'Donoghue, 125, Queen's Road, Cheetham, Manchester.
